target_flow: Create and manipulate 3D objects in Blender
critical_note: Blender renders its ENTIRE UI in OpenGL. UIA sees only the window (childCount: 0). No buttons, menus, or panels are accessible through the accessibility tree. ALL interaction must be through keyboard shortcuts. The mouse cursor MUST be over the 3D viewport for shortcuts to work (Blender is context-sensitive).

navigation_plan.note: CRITICAL: mouse must be over 3D viewport for shortcuts to work. Hover at roughly 40% x, 50% y of window.
All actions via keyboard. See shortcut reference below.

shortcut_reference.search: F3 — opens command search. Type command name, Enter to execute. This is the universal way to run any Blender command.

add_objects.add_via_search: F3 → type 'Add UV Sphere' / 'Add Cube' / 'Add Cylinder' / 'Add Cone' / 'Add Torus' → Enter

transform.grab_move: G (then optionally X/Y/Z to constrain axis, type number for distance, Enter to confirm)
transform.scale: S (then type number for scale factor, Enter to confirm)
transform.rotate: R (then type number for degrees, Enter to confirm)
transform.constrain_to_axis: After G/S/R, press X, Y, or Z to constrain

modes.object_mode: Tab (toggles between Object and Edit mode)

known_issues: UIA returns childCount: 0 — entire UI is OpenGL rendered with zero accessibility
ALL interaction must be keyboard-based — no element clicking possible
Mouse cursor position determines which panel receives keyboard shortcuts
Shift+A may not work via SendKeys — use F3 search as reliable alternative
Screenshots are the ONLY way to see what Blender is displaying
No text can be copied from the viewport — only screenshots for reading state
Blender 5.x may have different shortcuts than 4.x/3.x
achievement: First known AI agent to create 3D objects in Blender through desktop automation. Zero accessibility tree, pure keyboard control.
